what.title: What is Pyeong?

1 Pyeong = approximately 3.3058 m² (exactly 400/121 m²)
Commonly used in real estate and interior design
Officially m² is the standard, but Pyeong is still widely used

reference.title: Apartment Area Reference
items: Exclusive area: Actual usable space (rooms, living room, kitchen)
Supply area: Exclusive area + common residential area
Contract area: Supply area + other common areas (parking, management)
A '25-pyeong apartment' typically means about 82.6 m² in supply area

basic.title: What is VAT?
items: Value Added Tax (VAT) is a tax on the supply of goods and services
Korea's VAT rate is 10%
Supply Amount × 10% = VAT
Total = Supply Amount + VAT

reverse.title: Reverse VAT Calculation
items: From total: Supply Amount = Total ÷ 1.1
From total: VAT = Total ÷ 11
Tax invoices require separate supply amount and VAT
Simplified taxpayers have different VAT rates by industry (1.5%-4%)

alcoholCalculator.title: Blood Alcohol Calculator
alcoholCalculator.description: Estimate blood alcohol concentration (BAC) using the Widmark formula.
alcoholCalculator.warning: This calculator is for reference only and may differ from actual BAC. Never drive after drinking.

formula.title: Widmark Formula
items: BAC = (Alcohol(ml) × ABV × 0.7894) / (Weight(kg) × Body Water Ratio) - (Hours × 0.015)
Body water ratio: Male 0.68, Female 0.55
Alcohol metabolism rate: approximately 0.015% per hour
This formula provides estimates and individual results may vary

factors.title: Influencing Factors
items: Higher body weight results in lower BAC
Drinking on an empty stomach increases absorption and BAC
Women have lower body water ratio, resulting in higher BAC for same intake
Fatigue and medication can affect alcohol metabolism rate

example.title: Calculation Example

Monthly 1M (deposit 100M, rate 5%) → Jeonse: 100M + (1M × 12 ÷ 5%) = 340M

businessNumber.title: Business Registration Number Validator
businessNumber.description: Validate Korean business registration numbers using checksum algorithm
businessNumber.validMessage: This is a valid business registration number
businessNumber.invalidMessage: This is an invalid business registration number

structure.title: Business Number Structure
items: 10 digits total (XXX-XX-XXXXX format)
First 3 digits: Tax office code
Middle 2 digits: Individual/Corporate classification
Next 4 digits: Serial number
Last 1 digit: Check digit (checksum)

validation.title: Validation Algorithm
items: Uses weight array [1, 3, 7, 1, 3, 7, 1, 3, 5]
Multiply first 9 digits by respective weights and sum
For 9th position, also add Math.floor(digit × 5 / 10)
Subtract (sum mod 10) from 10, then take mod 10
Result should match the last (10th) digit

result.formula: Formula: Avg Daily Wage × 30 days × (Total Days ÷ 365)

calculation.title: Calculation Method
items: Severance = Avg Daily Wage × 30 × (Total Days ÷ 365)
Avg Daily Wage = Total wages for last 3 months ÷ days
Total wages = Base pay + Bonus(3/12) + Leave allowance(3/12)
Eligible after 1+ year of continuous employment

tips.title: Notes
items: Mandatory for 1+ year employment under Korean Labor Standards Act
Bonuses and leave allowances are included in average wage
Must be paid within 14 days of termination
Different calculation for DC/DB retirement pension plans

salaryComparison.title: Salary Comparison Calculator

items: Companies with more non-taxable benefits yield higher take-home pay even at the same salary.
Consider non-taxable allowances (meal: 200K KRW, vehicle maintenance) during salary negotiation.
More dependents reduce income tax and increase take-home pay.
Due to progressive tax rates, the take-home increase rate decreases as salary rises.

Health Insurance: 3.545%

inheritanceGiftTax.title: Inheritance & Gift Tax Calculator

inheritanceGiftTax.disclaimer: This calculator is for reference only. Actual taxes may vary depending on real estate valuation, prior gifts aggregation, generation-skipping surcharge, and other factors. Please consult a tax professional for accurate calculations.

inheritance.title: Inheritance Tax Deductions
items: The larger of the lump-sum deduction (500M) or itemized deduction (200M basic + personal) is automatically applied.
If a spouse exists, a minimum spouse deduction of 500M (up to 3B) is added.
With a spouse, up to 1B KRW (500M lump-sum + 500M spouse) is inheritance tax-free.
20% of net financial assets (min 20M, max 200M) is deducted as financial asset deduction.
Funeral expenses are deductible up to 15M KRW (10M direct costs + 5M cremation facility).

gift.title: Gift Tax Deductions
items: From spouse: 600M KRW deduction over 10 years
From parents to adult children: 50M KRW deduction over 10 years
From parents to minor children: 20M KRW deduction over 10 years
From children (direct descendants): 50M KRW deduction over 10 years
Since 2024, an additional 100M KRW deduction for marriage/childbirth gifts from direct ascendants (separate limit)

tips.title: Tax Saving Tips
items: Utilize the 10-year gift deduction cycle to significantly reduce taxes over the long term.
Start gifting to children early for compound growth benefits and tax savings simultaneously.
Use spousal gifts (600M deduction) to distribute assets before inheritance for potential savings.
Real estate is assessed at official price rather than market value, which may affect the actual burden.

freelancerTax.title: Freelancer Tax Calculator
freelancerTax.description: Compare 3.3% withholding tax with actual income tax to calculate expected refund or additional payment.

withholding.title: What is 3.3% Withholding?
items: When freelancers receive payment, 3% income tax + 0.3% local tax = 3.3% is withheld at source.
This is a 'prepaid tax' that gets settled during the annual tax filing in May.
If withholding > actual tax, you get a refund. If less, you pay the difference.
Income received without withholding must also be reported in the annual filing.

expenseRates.title: Expense Rate Selection Guide
items: Simple rate: Available if prior year revenue was under 24M KRW (non-professionals).
Standard rate: Applied to businesses above a certain size or those required to keep double-entry books.
Actual expenses: Enter real expenses with supporting documents for more accurate calculation.
Rates vary significantly by industry and are based on National Tax Service standards.

tips.title: Tax Saving Tips
items: Keep receipts for business expenses (transport, communication, equipment) to help reduce taxes.
National pension and health insurance are fully deductible - always include them in your filing.
If annual revenue exceeds 48M KRW, consider double-entry bookkeeping even if not required.
Freelancers can get up to 7M KRW tax credit through IRP (Individual Retirement Pension) contributions.

subnetCalculator.title: IP Subnet Calculator
subnetCalculator.description: Calculate IPv4 subnets easily. Check CIDR, subnet mask, and host range.

subnetCalculator.lookupPrompt: Enter CIDR ranges and IP addresses.

basics.title: Subnet Basics
items: A subnet divides a larger network into smaller segments.
The subnet mask separates the network portion from the host portion of an IP address.
The network address is the first address in the subnet (all host bits are 0).
The broadcast address is the last address in the subnet (all host bits are 1).
Usable hosts = total addresses - 2 (network + broadcast)

cidr.title: CIDR Notation
items: /24 = subnet mask 255.255.255.0 (254 hosts)
/8 = subnet mask 255.0.0.0 (~16.7 million hosts)
Higher numbers mean smaller networks; lower numbers mean larger networks.

htmlEntityConverter.title: HTML Entity Converter
htmlEntityConverter.description: Encode HTML special characters to entity codes or decode entities back to original characters.

what.title: What are HTML Entities?
items: HTML entities are escape codes used to safely represent special characters in HTML.
Characters like & < > " ' must be encoded as they conflict with HTML syntax.
Encoding user input is critical for preventing XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) attacks.

formats.title: Encoding Format Comparison
items: Named: © — Human-readable but only supports predefined characters
Decimal: © — Unicode code point in decimal, supports all characters
Hex: © — Unicode code point in hexadecimal, supports all characters
Named format only covers about 250 predefined entities.

usage.title: Usage Tips
items: Encoding is needed when embedding special characters in JavaScript or CSS within HTML.
Named entities have the best compatibility in HTML email templates.
When embedding HTML in JSON, also escape " and \ characters.
In RSS/XML feeds, & < > " ' are the 5 mandatory encoding targets.

exerciseCalorie.title: Exercise Calorie Calculator
exerciseCalorie.description: Calculate calories burned during exercise based on MET (Metabolic Equivalent of Task).
exerciseCalorie.metDescription: MET (Metabolic Equivalent of Task) measures exercise intensity. Resting energy expenditure = 1 MET. Calories = MET × weight(kg) × time(h). Source: Compendium of Physical Activities.
exerciseCalorie.inputPrompt: Enter your weight and add exercises to calculate calories burned.

formula.title: Calorie Formula
items: Calories (kcal) = MET × weight (kg) × time (hours)
Example: 70kg, 30min jogging (MET 7.0) → 7.0 × 70 × 0.5 = 245kcal
1kg body fat ≈ 7,700 kcal (basis for fat conversion)
1 bowl of rice ≈ 300 kcal

tips.title: Effective Exercise Tips
items: High-intensity exercises (running, jump rope) burn more calories in the same time.
Strength training increases metabolic rate even after exercise (EPOC effect).
Combining cardio and strength training is effective for weight loss and muscle maintenance.
Fasted cardio may help fat burning, but high intensity risks muscle loss.

formula.title: Pension Calculation Formula
items: Basic Pension = 1.2 × (A + B) × (contribution months / 480)
A value = average income of all subscribers in the 3 years before pension eligibility (~2.86M KRW in 2024)
B value = average standard monthly income during your contribution period
5% additional payment for every 12 months beyond 20 years of contributions
This calculator assumes your current income remains constant throughout

cssUnitConverter.title: CSS Unit Converter
cssUnitConverter.description: Instantly convert between CSS units: px, rem, em, vw, vh, %, pt, cm, mm, in.
cssUnitConverter.quickRefDesc: Quick reference table for frequently used CSS unit conversions.
guideAbsoluteItems: px (pixels): Smallest screen unit. Fixed size. On retina displays, 1px maps to 2–3 physical pixels.
cm / mm / in: Physical size units. Mainly used in print CSS rather than screen layouts.
guideRelativeItems: rem: Relative to the root (<html>) font size. Default is usually 16px. Great for global scaling.
em: Relative to the current or parent element's font size. Can compound when nested — use carefully.
% (percent): Relative to the parent element's corresponding property. For font-size, % is relative to parent font size.
guideViewportItems: vw: 1% of the viewport width. 100vw = full screen width. Ideal for responsive layouts.
vh: 1% of the viewport height. 100vh = full screen height. Great for full-screen sections.
svh/dvh: New viewport units addressing mobile browser address bar issues (modern browser support).
guideTipsItems: Font sizes: Use rem — respects user browser settings and improves accessibility.
Layout spacing: Use rem or px — maintains consistency.
Component inner spacing: Use em — scales with the component's font size.
Responsive widths: Use vw, %, or min/max combinations for flexible layouts.
Media query breakpoints: Use px or em — em responds to user font size preferences.
Print CSS: Use pt, cm, mm — guarantees physical dimensions.

vs.title: Comparing lump sum vs DCA with the same total amount

concepts.title: Key Concepts
items: CAGR: Compound Annual Growth Rate, assumes steady annual growth
Lump Sum: Investing all money at once for long-term holding
DCA: Dollar Cost Averaging, investing fixed amounts monthly
Real Return: Actual purchasing power return after deducting inflation

formulas.title: Formulas
items: Compound: FV = PV × (1 + r/12)^(12×n)
CAGR = (Final/Initial)^(1/years) - 1
Real Return = (1+Nominal)/(1+Inflation) - 1

tips.title: Investment Tips
items: Lump sum tends to outperform DCA over longer periods

warning.title: Not Eligible for Holiday Pay
warning.message: Workers with less than 15 hours per week are not eligible for weekly holiday pay. You must work 15 or more hours per week.

info.title: What is Weekly Holiday Pay?
items: Workers who work 15+ hours per week are entitled to 1 paid day off (Labor Standards Act Art. 55)
Holiday hours = (total weekly hours ÷ 40) × 8 hours (max 8 hours)
Holiday pay = hourly wage × holiday hours
Applies regardless of social insurance enrollment if 15+ hours per week
Failure to pay holiday pay constitutes wage theft

calc.title: Calculation Method
items: Total weekly hours = daily hours × working days
Holiday hours = (weekly hours ÷ 40) × 8, max 8 hours
Holiday pay = hourly wage × holiday hours
Monthly pay = (weekly base + holiday pay) × 4.345
Annual pay = monthly pay × 12

notes.title: Important Notes
items: Must have perfect attendance on scheduled workdays in the week
Ultra-short-time workers (avg. < 15 hrs/week over 4 weeks) are excluded
Freelancers and self-employed are not eligible (based on employment contract)
For 5 days × 8 hours (40 hrs), maximum holiday hours is 8
Holiday pay applies during probation period as well

howToUse.title: How to Use
steps: Upload your image by dragging it onto the upload area or clicking to browse.
Click the 'Pick Background Color' button, then click on the background in the original image.
Adjust the tolerance slider — higher values remove more similar colors.
Use edge softening to smooth out the edges of the cutout.
When satisfied, click 'Download Transparent PNG' to save your result.

tips.title: Tips & Recommendations
items: Works best with solid-color backgrounds (white backgrounds, green screen, etc.).
Try clicking different parts of the background and adjust tolerance for better results.
Edge softening of 1–2px creates a natural-looking edge.
Lower the tolerance when the subject has similar colors to the background.
The checkerboard pattern in the result represents transparency — actual PNG will have no background.

limitations.title: Limitations & Notes
items: Gradient or patterned backgrounds are difficult to remove completely.
Color-based removal may accidentally remove parts of the subject if it shares colors with the background.
Large high-resolution images may take a few seconds to process.
Unlike AI-based tools (remove.bg), this uses simple color comparison and works best on uniform backgrounds.

whatIsToken.title: What is a Token?
items: Tokens are the basic units LLMs use to process text
In English, ~4 characters ≈ 1 token; in Korean, ~1-2 characters ≈ 1 token
The same text may have different token counts depending on the model's tokenizer
Special characters, spaces, and line breaks also count as tokens

koreanTokens.title: Korean Token Characteristics
items: Korean is less token-efficient than English, consuming more tokens
Claude has relatively good Korean token efficiency
Frequently used Korean words require fewer tokens
Mixing English can reduce overall token count

capitalGainsTax.guideTitle: How Capital Gains Tax is Calculated
guideSteps: Capital Gain = Sale Price − Acquisition Price − Expenses
Apply long-term holding deduction (if held 3+ years)
Taxable Gain = Capital Gain − Long-Term Deduction
Tax Base = Taxable Gain − Basic Deduction
Calculated Tax = Tax Base × Rate − Progressive Deduction
Total Tax = Calculated Tax + Local Income Tax
guideExemptItems: Regulated area: also requires 2+ years of actual residence
Sale price ≤ KRW 1.2 billion: fully exempt
Sale price > KRW 1.2 billion: only the excess portion is taxed
Only applies when a household owns exactly one home

Compare with prepaid tax to determine refund/additional payment
yearEndTaxCalc.guide.changes.title: 2025 Tax Year Key Changes
guide.changes.items: Child tax credit increased: 1 child 250K, 2 children 550K
Rent credit limit raised to 10M, salary threshold to 80M
Marriage tax credit 500K newly introduced (2024-2026 registration)
Housing subscription limit raised to 3M, spouse now eligible
Medical expense limit removed for children under 6
Childcare allowance non-taxable limit raised to 200K/month
Gym/swimming pool expenses now eligible for 30% card deduction
yearEndTaxCalc.guide.tips.title: Tax Saving Tips
guide.tips.items: Spending over 25% of salary: debit cards/cash receipts get 2x rate (30%)
Pension savings + IRP: up to 9M eligible for tax credit
Medical expenses: only amount exceeding 3% of salary is deductible—concentrate in one year
Traditional markets and public transport each have separate additional 1M limit
